exports.content: The DFT predictions for high-Tc superconductivity in clathrate superhydrides are experimentally validated: LaH₁₀ achieves Tc = 250 K in the predicted Fm-3m structure, confirming that crystal structure prediction methods can reliably identify high-temperature superconductors. The predicted Tc ~ 280 K at 200 GPa is reasonably close to the observed 250 K at 170 GPa.
exports.label: dft_clathrate_prediction_validated

exports.content: The Fm-3m face-centered cubic clathrate structure of LaH₁₀ is confirmed by XRD at 150 GPa. The lattice constant a = 5.1019 Å and estimated stoichiometry LaH₉.₆ are in excellent agreement with DFT predictions for the sodalite-like H₃₂ cage structure. This is the first structural confirmation of a predicted clathrate superhydride.
exports.label: lah10_structure_confirmed

exports.content: Room-temperature superconductivity (273 K) appears achievable in hydrogen-rich materials at high pressure. The leap of ~50 K above the H₃S record (203 K → 250 K) demonstrates rapid progress. Theoretical predictions for YH₁₀ (Tc = 303-326 K at 250 GPa) motivate further experiments. Carbon-based materials with high hydrogen content offer a potential path toward ambient-pressure superconductivity.
exports.label: rtsc_prospect

exports.content: Genuine superconductivity at 250 K in LaH₁₀ is confirmed by three independent lines of evidence: (1) sharp resistance drop to zero in multiple samples, (2) systematic suppression of Tc under magnetic fields up to 9 T with extrapolated Hc2(0) = 95-136 T, and (3) a strong isotope effect with α ≈ 0.57 close to the BCS value of 0.5, confirming phonon-mediated pairing.
exports.label: superconductivity_confirmed
